---
title: "Research"
---

## Residual Stream Geometry

### State-lifetime geometry in transformer residual streams

Transformers process text one token at a time, but they do not have an explicit recurrent state. This project asks whether the residual stream nevertheless contains directions that behave like persistent state: directions whose scalar projections remain autocorrelated across many token positions.

The current pilot studies the layer-12 residual stream of Gemma-2-2B on C4 documents. It compares random directions, PCA directions, and time-lagged directions optimized for temporal stability.

The main finding is that persistence is compact, sequential, and geometrically nontrivial:

- Time-lagged directions reveal a heavy tail of residual-stream lifetimes.
- The signal collapses under document-level token permutation.
- Around 31 nonredundant directions account for most lifetime excess.
- The persistent directions are rotations inside the high-variance PCA span, not low-variance hidden directions.
- Raw attention alignment and high variance are not enough; persistent directions show attention-specific geometric excess beyond residual PCA.

The public writeup is here:

### [The Residual Stream Has a Geometry of Time](notes/residual-geometry.qmd)

The post is written as a technical blog report: enough methods to audit the result, enough figures to understand the geometry, and explicit boundaries around what is not yet established causally.

## Next Questions

- Validate the qualitative register/template interpretation on held-out data with predefined labels.
- Test whether the same persistent subspace appears across layers.
- Run prospectively locked causal routing and ablation tests.
- Replicate the analysis across model families.
- Connect persistent directions to sparse autoencoder features.
